title: Active area elements must have alternate text
description: Ensures area elements of image maps have alternate text

title: Elements must only use allowed ARIA attributes
description: Ensures ARIA attributes are allowed for an element's role

title: All ARIA command role elements must have an accessible name
description: Ensures every ARIA button link and menuitem role element has an accessible name

title: aria-hidden attribute must not be present on the document body
description: Ensures aria-hidden attribute is not present on the document body element

title: ARIA hidden element must not contain focusable elements
description: Ensures that aria-hidden elements do not contain focusable elements

title: ARIA input fields must have an accessible name
description: Ensures every ARIA input role element has an accessible name

title: ARIA meter nodes must have an accessible name
description: Ensures every ARIA meter node has an accessible name

title: ARIA progressbar nodes must have an accessible name
description: Ensures every ARIA progressbar node has an accessible name

title: Required ARIA attributes must be provided
description: Ensures elements with ARIA roles have all required ARIA attributes

title: Certain ARIA roles must contain particular children
description: Ensures elements with an ARIA role that require child roles contain them

title: Certain ARIA roles must be contained by particular parents
description: Ensures elements with an ARIA role that require parent roles are contained by them

title: All ARIA roles must be valid
description: Ensures all elements with a role attribute use a valid value

title: ARIA toggle field elements must have an accessible name
description: Ensures every ARIA toggle field element has an accessible name

title: ARIA tooltip nodes must have an accessible name
description: Ensures every ARIA tooltip node has an accessible name

title: ARIA treeitem nodes must have an accessible name
description: Ensures every ARIA treeitem node has an accessible name

title: ARIA attributes must conform to valid names
description: Ensures attributes that begin with aria- are valid ARIA attributes

title: ARIA attributes must conform to valid values
description: Ensures all ARIA attributes have valid values

title: Buttons must have discernible text
description: Ensures buttons have discernible text

title: Page must contain a skip navigation link
description: Ensures each page and document has at least one mechanism for a user to bypass navigation

title: Elements must have sufficient color contrast
description: Ensures the contrast between foreground and background colors meets WCAG 2 AA minimum contrast ratio thresholds

title: Elements must have sufficient color contrast (Level AAA)
description: Ensures the contrast between foreground and background colors meets WCAG 2 AAA enhanced contrast ratio thresholds

title: dl elements must only directly contain properly-ordered dt and dd groups
description: Ensures dl elements are structured correctly

title: dt and dd elements must be contained by a dl
description: Ensures dt and dd elements are contained by a dl

title: Documents must have a non-empty title element
description: Ensures each HTML document contains a non-empty title element

title: id attribute value must be unique
description: Ensures every id attribute value is unique

title: IDs of active interactive elements must be unique
description: Ensures every id attribute value of active elements is unique

title: IDs used in ARIA and labels must be unique
description: Ensures every id attribute value used in ARIA and in labels is unique

title: Form fields must not have multiple labels
description: Ensures form field does not have multiple label elements

title: Frames with focusable content must not have tabindex=-1
description: Ensures frame or iframe elements with focusable content do not have tabindex=-1

title: Frames must have an accessible name
description: Ensures frame and iframe elements have an accessible name

title: html element must have a lang attribute
description: Ensures every HTML document has a lang attribute

title: html element must have a valid value for the lang attribute
description: Ensures the lang attribute of the html element has a valid value

title: HTML elements with both lang and xml:lang attributes must have the same base language
description: Ensures that HTML elements with both valid lang and xml:lang attributes agree on the base language of the page

title: Images must have alternate text
description: Ensures img elements have alternate text or a role of none or presentation

title: Image alternative text must not be redundant to adjacent text
description: Ensure image alternative is not repeated as text

title: Input buttons must have discernible text
description: Ensures input buttons have discernible text

title: Image buttons must have alternate text
description: Ensures input elements with type=image have alternate text

title: All interactive elements must be navigable via keyboard
description: Ensures all functionality is accessible using only the keyboard

title: Form elements must have labels
description: Ensures every form element has a label

title: Elements with visible text label must have an accessible name that includes the visible text label
description: Ensures that elements labelled through their content must have their visible text as part of their accessible name

title: Banner landmark must not be nested in another landmark
description: Ensures the banner role is used correctly so that all users can benefit from the functionality it provides

title: Aside elements must be top-level unless they are nestable
description: Ensures the complementary role is used correctly so that all users can benefit

title: Contentinfo landmark must not be nested in another landmark
description: Ensures the contentinfo role is used correctly

title: Main landmark must not be nested in another landmark
description: Ensures the main role attribute is used correctly

title: Document must not have more than one banner landmark
description: Ensures the document has at most one banner landmark

title: Document must not have more than one contentinfo landmark
description: Ensures the document has at most one contentinfo landmark

title: Document must not have more than one main landmark
description: Ensures the document has at most one main landmark

title: Document must have one main landmark
description: Ensures the document has a main landmark

title: Landmarks should have a unique role or role/label/title combination
description: Ensures landmarks are unique

title: Links must be distinguishable without relying on color
description: Ensure links are distinguished from surrounding text in a way that does not rely on color

title: Links must have discernible text
description: Ensures links have discernible text

title: list element must only directly contain allowed elements
description: Ensures that lists are structured correctly

title: List item elements must be contained in a list or group role
description: Ensures li elements are used semantically

title: Ensure that time-based content does not automatically redirect or refresh
description: Ensures meta-refresh is not used for delayed action

title: zoom and maximum-scale in the viewport meta tag must not be disabled
description: Ensures meta-viewport does not disable text scaling and zooming

title: Interactive controls must not be nested
description: Ensures interactive controls are not nested as they are not always announced by screen readers

title: An element that plays audio must not auto play for more than 3 seconds
description: Ensures audio or video elements do not autoplay audio for more than 3 seconds without providing an audio control mechanism to turn off or control the volume

title: Object elements must have alternate text
description: Ensures object elements have alternate text

title: Bold italicized and font-size styled text must not act as a visual heading
description: Ensure bold italicized and font-size styled text is not used to replace heading markup

title: Page must contain a level-one heading
description: Ensure that the page or at least one of its frames contains a level-one heading

title: All page content should be contained by landmarks
description: Ensures all page content is contained by landmarks

title: Images with role=img must have an alternate text
description: Ensures role=img elements have alternate text

title: scope attribute values must be used correctly
description: Ensures the scope attribute is used correctly on tables

title: Elements that have scrollable content must be accessible by keyboard
description: Elements that have scrollable content must be accessible by keyboard

title: Select element must have an accessible name
description: Ensures select element has an accessible name

title: Avoid server-side image maps
description: Ensures that server-side image maps are not used

title: The page must have a means to bypass repeated blocks
description: Ensure all skip links have a focusable target

title: SVG images must have an alternate text
description: Ensures SVG elements with an img role have an accessible text

title: Elements should not have tabindex greater than zero
description: Ensures that tabindex attribute values are not greater than 0

title: The summary attribute must not duplicate the caption element
description: Ensures the summarya attribute does not duplicate the caption element

title: All cells in a table element that use the headers attribute must only refer to other cells in that table
description: Ensures that each cell using the headers refers to another cell in the same table

title: All th elements and elements with role=columnheader/rowheader must have data cells they describe
description: Ensures that th elements and elements with role=columnheader/rowheader have data cells they describe

title: lang attributes must have a valid value
description: Ensures lang attributes have valid values

title: Video elements must have captions
description: Ensures video elements have captions

title: CSS Media Queries must not lock orientation of the screen
description: Ensures content is not locked to any specific display orientation and the content is operable in all display orientations

title: Page must not trap focus
description: Ensures users cannot be trapped in a content area through keyboard input

title: Some content is not visible and may not be tested for accessibility
description: Informs users about hidden content present on the page that may need to be manually inspected

title: Links with the same accessible name must serve a similar purpose
description: Ensure that links with the same accessible name serve the same purpose

title: Landmarks must be on a section of the page with its body context
description: Ensures landmarks only appear in the context of a body element

title: Users should be able to zoom and scale the text up to 500%
description: Ensures meta-viewport allows users to scale text up to 500%

title: Elements with role=presentation or role=none must not have ARIA attributes
description: Flags elements that have conflicting roles and must be resolved

title: Ensure touch target have sufficient size
description: Ensure touch targets have an accessible size

title: Ensure the autocomplete attribute is correct
description: Ensure the autocomplete attribute is correct and suitable for the form field it is used with

title: Inline text spacing must be adjustable with custom stylesheets
description: Ensure that text spacing set through style attributes can be adjusted with custom stylesheets

title: Headings should not be empty
description: Ensures headings have discernible text

title: Table header text should not be empty
description: Ensures table headers have discernible text

title: Heading levels should only increase by one
description: Ensures the order of headings is semantically correct

title: Non-text Content
description: All non-text content that is presented to the user has a text alternative that serves the equivalent purpose, except for certain situations.

title: Audio-only and Video-only (Prerecorded)
description: For prerecorded audio-only and prerecorded video-only media, the following are true, except when the audio or video is a media alternative for text.

title: Captions (Prerecorded)
description: Captions are provided for all prerecorded audio content in synchronized media, except when the media is a media alternative for text.

title: Audio Description or Media Alternative (Prerecorded)
description: An alternative for time-based media or audio description of the prerecorded video content is provided for synchronized media, except when the media is a media alternative for text.

title: Captions (Live)
description: Captions are provided for all live audio content in synchronized media.

title: Audio Description (Prerecorded)
description: Audio description is provided for all prerecorded video content in synchronized media.

title: Sign Language (Prerecorded)
description: Sign language interpretation is provided for all prerecorded audio content in synchronized media.

title: Extended Audio Description (Prerecorded)
description: Where pauses in foreground audio are insufficient to allow audio descriptions to convey the sense of the video, extended audio description is provided for all prerecorded video content in synchronized media.

title: Media Alternative (Prerecorded)
description: An alternative for time-based media is provided for all prerecorded synchronized media and for all prerecorded video-only media.

title: Audio-only (Live)
description: An alternative for time-based media that presents equivalent information for live audio-only content is provided.

title: Info and Relationships
description: Information, structure, and relationships conveyed through presentation can be programmatically determined or are available in text.

title: Meaningful Sequence
description: If the sequence in which content is presented affects its meaning, a correct reading sequence can be programmatically determined.

title: Sensory Characteristics
description: Instructions provided for understanding and operating content do not rely solely on sensory characteristics of components such as shape, color, size, visual location, orientation, or sound.

title: Orientation
description: Content does not restrict its view and operation to a single display orientation, such as portrait or landscape, unless a specific display orientation is essential.

title: Identify Input Purpose
description: The purpose of each input field collecting information about the user can be programmatically determined when the input field serves a purpose identified in the Input Purposes for User Interface Components section.

title: Identify Purpose
description: In content implemented using markup languages, the purpose of User Interface Components, icons, and regions can be programmatically determined.

title: Use of Color
description: Color is not used as the only visual means of conveying information indicating an action prompting a response or distinguishing a visual element.

title: Audio Control
description: If any audio on a Web page plays automatically for more than 3 seconds either a mechanism is available to pause or stop the audio or a mechanism is available to control audio volume independently from the overall system volume level.

title: Contrast (Minimum)
description: The visual presentation of text and images of text has a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1 except for large text incidental text and logotypes.

title: Resize Text
description: Except for captions and images of text, text can be resized without assistive technology up to 200 percent without loss of content or functionality.

title: Images of Text
description: If the technologies being used can achieve the visual presentation, text is used to convey information rather than images of text except for customizable and essential cases.

title: Contrast (Enhanced)
description: The visual presentation of text and images of text has a contrast ratio of at least 7:1 except for large text incidental text and logotypes.

title: Low or No Background Audio
description: For prerecorded audio-only content that contains primarily speech in the foreground, is not an audio CAPTCHA or audio logo, and is not vocalization intended to be primarily musical expression such as singing or rapping, at least one of the following is true.

title: Visual Presentation
description: For the visual presentation of blocks of text a mechanism is available to achieve certain visual presentation characteristics.

title: Images of Text (No Exception)
description: Images of text are only used for pure decoration or where a particular presentation of text is essential to the information being conveyed.

title: Reflow
description: Content can be presented without loss of information or functionality, and without requiring scrolling in two dimensions for vertical scrolling content at a width equivalent to 320 CSS pixels.

title: Non-text Contrast
description: The visual presentation of user interface components and graphical objects has a contrast ratio of at least 3:1 against adjacent color(s), except for inactive components or where the appearance of the component is determined by the user agent and not modified by the author.

title: Text Spacing
description: In content implemented using markup languages, no loss of content or functionality occurs by setting letter spacing line height and spacing after paragraphs to specified values.

title: Content on Hover or Focus
description: Where receiving and then removing pointer hover or keyboard focus triggers additional content to become visible and then hidden, the following are true.

title: Keyboard
description: All functionality of the content is operable through a keyboard interface without requiring specific timings for individual keystrokes, except where the underlying function requires input that depends on the path of the user's movement and not just the endpoints.

title: No Keyboard Trap
description: If keyboard focus can be moved to a component of the page using a keyboard interface, then focus can be moved away from that component using only a keyboard interface.

title: Keyboard (No Exception)
description: All functionality of the content is operable through a keyboard interface without requiring specific timings for individual keystrokes.

title: Character Key Shortcuts
description: If a keyboard shortcut is implemented in content using only letter (including upper and lower-case letters), punctuation, number, or symbol characters, then at least one of the following is true.

title: Timing Adjustable
description: For each time limit that is set by the content, at least one of the following is true.

title: Pause Stop Hide
description: For moving, blinking, scrolling, or auto-updating information, all of the following are true.

title: No Timing
description: Timing is not an essential part of the event or activity presented by the content except for non-interactive synchronized media and real-time events.

title: Interruptions
description: Interruptions can be postponed or suppressed by the user except interruptions involving an emergency.

title: Re-authenticating
description: When an authenticated session expires, the user can continue the activity without loss of data after re-authenticating.

title: Timeouts
description: Users are warned of the duration of any user inactivity that could cause data loss, unless the data is preserved for more than 20 hours when the user does not take any actions.

title: Three Flashes or Below Threshold
description: Web pages do not contain anything that flashes more than three times in any one second period or the flash is below the general flash and red flash thresholds.

title: Three Flashes
description: Web pages do not contain anything that flashes more than three times in any one second period.

title: Animation from Interactions
description: Motion animation triggered by interaction can be disabled unless the animation is essential to the functionality or the information being conveyed.

title: Bypass Blocks
description: A mechanism is available to bypass blocks of content that are repeated on multiple Web pages.

title: Page Titled
description: Web pages have titles that describe topic or purpose.

title: Focus Order
description: If a Web page can be navigated sequentially and the navigation sequences affect meaning or operation, focusable components receive focus in an order that preserves meaning and operability.

title: Link Purpose (In Context)
description: The purpose of each link can be determined from the link text alone or from the link text together with its programmatically determined link context, except where the purpose of the link would be ambiguous to users in general.

title: Multiple Ways
description: More than one way is available to locate a Web page within a set of Web pages except where the Web Page is the result of or a step in a process.

title: Headings and Labels
description: Headings and labels describe topic or purpose.

title: Focus Visible
description: Any keyboard operable user interface has a mode of operation where the keyboard focus indicator is visible.

title: Location
description: Information about the user's location within a set of Web pages is available.

title: Link Purpose (Link Only)
description: A mechanism is available to allow the purpose of each link to be identified from link text alone, except where the purpose of the link would be ambiguous to users in general.

title: Section Headings
description: Section headings are used to organize the content.

title: Pointer Gestures
description: All functionality that uses multipoint or path-based gestures for operation can be operated with a single pointer without a path-based gesture, unless a multipoint or path-based gesture is essential.

title: Pointer Cancellation
description: For functionality that can be operated using a single pointer, at least one of the following is true.

title: Label in Name
description: For user interface components with labels that include text or images of text, the name contains the text that is presented visually.

title: Motion Actuation
description: Functionality that can be operated by device motion or user motion can also be operated by user interface components and responding to the motion can be disabled to prevent accidental actuation.

title: Target Size
description: The size of the target for pointer inputs is at least 44 by 44 CSS pixels.

title: Concurrent Input Mechanisms
description: Web content does not restrict use of input modalities available on a platform except where the restriction is essential.

title: Language of Page
description: The default human language of each Web page can be programmatically determined.

title: Language of Parts
description: The human language of each passage or phrase in the content can be programmatically determined except for proper names, technical terms, words of indeterminate language, and words or phrases that have become part of the vernacular of the immediately surrounding text.

title: Unusual Words
description: A mechanism is available for identifying specific definitions of words or phrases used in an unusual or restricted way including idioms and jargon.

title: Abbreviations
description: A mechanism for identifying the expanded form or meaning of abbreviations is available.

title: Reading Level
description: When text requires reading ability more advanced than the lower secondary education level after removal of proper names and titles, supplemental content, or a version that does not require reading ability more advanced than the lower secondary education level, is available.

title: Pronunciation
description: A mechanism is available for identifying the specific pronunciation of words where the meaning of the words in context is ambiguous without knowing the pronunciation.

title: On Focus
description: If any component receives focus it does not initiate a change of context.

title: On Input
description: Changing the setting of any user interface component does not automatically cause a change of context unless the user has been advised of the behavior before using the component.

title: Consistent Navigation
description: Navigational mechanisms that are repeated on multiple Web pages within a set of Web pages occur in the same relative order each time they are repeated, unless a change is initiated by the user.

title: Consistent Identification
description: Components that have the same functionality within a set of Web pages are identified consistently.

title: Change on Request
description: Changes of context are initiated only by user request or a mechanism is available to turn off such changes.

title: Error Identification
description: If an input error is automatically detected, the item that is in error is identified and the error is described to the user in text.

title: Labels or Instructions
description: Labels or instructions are provided when content requires user input.

title: Error Suggestion
description: If an input error is automatically detected and suggestions for correction are known, then the suggestion is provided to the user, unless it would jeopardize the security or purpose of the content.

title: Error Prevention (Legal Financial Data)
description: For Web pages that cause legal commitments or financial transactions for the user to occur, that modify or delete user-controllable data in data storage systems, or that submit user test responses, at least one of the following is true.

title: Help
description: Context-sensitive help is available.

title: Error Prevention (All)
description: For Web pages that require the user to submit information, at least one of the following is true.

title: Parsing
description: In content implemented using markup languages, elements have complete start and end tags, elements are nested according to their specifications, elements do not contain duplicate attributes, and any IDs are unique, except where the specifications allow these features.

title: Name Role Value
description: For all user interface components (including but not limited to: form elements, links and components generated by scripts), the name and role can be programmatically determined; states, properties, and values that can be set by the user can be programmatically determined; and notification of changes to these items is available to user agents, including assistive technologies.

title: Status Messages
description: In content implemented using markup languages, status messages can be programmatically determined through role or properties such that they can be presented to the user by assistive technologies without receiving focus.

title: The WebAIM Million: 2025 Accessibility Report
description: WebAIM's annual analysis of the top 1 million home pages reveals the current state of web accessibility. 96.3% of home pages had detectable WCAG 2 failures. The most common were low color contrast (81%), missing alternative text (55%), empty links (48%), missing form labels (44%), and empty buttons (28%).

title: Color Contrast: Why It Still Fails at Scale
description: Despite being the most well-known accessibility requirement, color contrast remains the top WCAG failure across the web. This analysis examines why teams keep shipping contrast violations and what automated tools catch vs miss.

title: ADA Digital Accessibility Lawsuits: 2024 Year in Review
description: Digital accessibility lawsuits reached a new high in 2024. This report analyzes trends, most-sued industries, and what failure patterns drove the most litigation.

title: Screen Reader User Survey #10 Results
description: Results of WebAIM's tenth screen reader user survey. Covers screen reader usage trends, browser combinations, and most-reported barriers for screen reader users in 2025.

title: WCAG 2.2: What Changed and What It Means for Your Site
description: An overview of the new success criteria introduced in WCAG 2.2, how they differ from 2.1, and practical guidance for teams updating their compliance targets.

title: Accessible Forms: The Most Common Mistakes in 2025
description: Forms remain one of the most failure-prone areas for web accessibility. This article covers the five most common form-related WCAG failures and how to fix each one.

title: Understanding ARIA Live Regions
description: A practical guide to aria-live, aria-atomic, and aria-relevant. When to use live regions, which values to choose, and common mistakes that break screen reader announcements.

title: Testing with Real Screen Readers in 2025
description: How to set up and test with NVDA, JAWS, and VoiceOver. Covers the most important test cases, keyboard navigation patterns, and how automated tools compare to manual screen reader testing.

title: Focus Management in Single-Page Applications
description: SPAs break the browser's natural focus management. This guide covers how to manage focus during route changes, modal dialogs, and dynamic content updates to maintain a usable keyboard experience.

title: Everything You Need to Know About Color Contrast
description: A comprehensive guide to WCAG 1.4.3 and 1.4.11. Covers contrast ratios, tools for checking contrast, common pitfalls with gradients and images, and how to handle edge cases.

title: How Much Can Automated Accessibility Testing Catch?
description: Automated tools like axe-core and Lighthouse catch about 30-40% of WCAG failures. This article explains what they catch, what they miss, and how to combine automated and manual testing for better coverage.

title: Cognitive Accessibility: Beyond WCAG Technical Requirements
description: WCAG addresses some cognitive accessibility needs but not all. This article covers reading level, plain language, consistent navigation, and error prevention patterns that help users with cognitive disabilities.

title: Skip Navigation Links: Still Essential in 2024
description: Skip links remain one of the most effective keyboard accessibility improvements. This guide covers implementation, styling (visible vs hidden), and testing to ensure they actually work.

title: PDF Accessibility: The Forgotten Compliance Gap
description: PDFs are one of the most inaccessible document formats in common use. This report covers the most common PDF accessibility failures and tools for remediation.

title: Mobile Accessibility Testing Checklist 2025
description: A practical checklist for testing mobile web accessibility on iOS and Android. Covers VoiceOver, TalkBack, touch target sizes, zoom behavior, and orientation lock testing.

title: Five WCAG Myths That Won't Die
description: Common misconceptions about WCAG compliance — including the belief that automated audits equal compliance, that WCAG applies only to government sites, and that accessibility is expensive to retrofit.

title: Accessibility Overlays: What the Research Says
description: An evidence-based review of accessibility overlay tools. Examines whether overlays improve WCAG compliance and presents user research on how disabled users experience overlay-equipped sites.

title: Writing Good Captions for Video Content
description: Guidance on caption accuracy, timing, speaker identification, and sound descriptions. Covers WCAG 1.2.2 requirements and tools for generating and editing captions.

title: Using Design Tokens for Accessible Color Systems
description: How to structure design tokens to ensure color contrast is maintained across themes, dark mode, and brand color updates. Covers Figma token setup and automated contrast checking in CI/CD.
